cards.title: A Kilometre Below The Waves
cards.caption: Attenborough narrating clownfish teamwork and life a kilometre under the ocean. Nobody makes nature feel this alive. What's the clip you rewatch every time? Surfaced on Threadverse.

cards.title: One Tiger vs A Wolf Pack
cards.caption: Siberia in 4K: the world's most ferocious predators, one frame. A lone tiger squaring off against a wolf pack. Who's your money on? Pulled from the noise by Threadverse.

cards.title: Those Eyes Are Watching You
cards.caption: Poison dart frogs raising tadpoles in rainforest pools, spiders hunting with terrifying agility. The hidden forest is stranger than fiction. Curated on Threadverse.
cards.povText: POV: you locked eyes with the forest and it blinked first

cards.title: The Spider That Hunts Rats
cards.caption: A spider big enough to take down a rat. The most lethal creatures on Earth, one documentary. Not for the arachnophobes. Found by Threadverse.

cards.title: Every Rustle Tells A Story
cards.caption: Marmots on the rocks, the incredible wildlife of hidden forests. Wholesome but a little too gentle to stop the scroll. Surfaced on Threadverse.

cards.title: The Full Jungles Episode Is Here
cards.caption: Our Planet, Jungles, full episode. Baby orangutan front and center, climate reality woven through breathtaking footage. The one everyone means to watch. Curated by Threadverse.

cards.title: Two Bear Cubs, One Mega Episode
cards.caption: Nat Geo's America's National Parks, the full season in one mega episode. These two cubs set the tone. What's the park you'd drop everything to see? Found on Threadverse.

cards.title: Baby Penguins Are Fully In Charge
cards.caption: A whole journey through the baby animal kingdom, puppies, cubs and these fluffy penguin chicks. The most heartwarming corner of nature. Kept by Threadverse.
cards.povText: POV: a baby penguin just became your entire personality

cards.title: Bison Sat On The Car
cards.caption: A full-grown bison decided the hood of a sedan was a good place to lean. Nobody's insurance covers this. What's the wildest animal-vs-car moment you've seen?
cards.povText: POV: your car just became the bison's chair

cards.title: Something Came Up The Line
cards.caption: Fishermen hauled up something the ocean clearly wanted to keep. Blurry, low-context clip — one of the ~4,950 posts Threadverse skipped today.

cards.title: Tourists Beware
cards.caption: Generic 'animal attack' compilation thumbnail with all the hashtag spam. Pure clickbait — the kind of noise Threadverse filters out.

cards.title: Do Not Run From A Moose
cards.caption: Hiker rounds a bend and a moose is RIGHT there. Reminder: they're bigger and faster than they look. Ever had a trail encounter that made you freeze?
cards.povText: POV: you round the trail and the moose was already there

cards.title: Hyenas Circled The Jeep
cards.caption: A pack of hyenas surrounds a safari jeep while someone leans WAY too far out the window. Bold move. Would you keep filming or roll the glass up?

cards.title: A Lion In The Backyard
cards.caption: A full-grown lion strolling past a kid on a suburban patio. Kangaroos on porches, gators on golf courses — nature's forgetting the property line. Where's the wildest sighting you've heard of?
cards.povText: POV: you look up from the patio and it's a lion

cards.title: Something Moved In The Yard
cards.caption: Night-cam catches a huge snake sliding through the yard behind someone who has no idea. Creepy, but low-res and low-context — Threadverse left this one on the cutting room floor.

cards.title: Ranking Her Scary Trail Run-Ins
cards.caption: An Appalachian Trail thru-hiker ranks her scariest animal encounters — and #6 is a grouse. If you know, you know. What would top YOUR trail list?

cards.title: Too Close To A Brown Bear
cards.caption: Photographing a "small group of relaxed" Alaskan brown bears in Chinitna Bay. Relaxed is doing a LOT of work in that sentence. Would you take this shot? One of ~50 our feed kept today.
cards.povText: POV: the bears are relaxed and you are not

cards.title: Already Waiting For Him
cards.caption: House lions ambushing their owner at midnight is a genre I didn't know I needed. Cats have been planning this since 3am. Tag someone whose cat does this. Surfaced by Threadverse.
cards.povText: POV: it's midnight and your cat has been strategizing

cards.title: Tourists Run From Charging Elephant
cards.caption: Two tourists narrowly outran a charging wild elephant on Highway 766 through Bandipur. This is your reminder that they are NOT a photo op. Caught early in our feed.

cards.title: Mothers Who Fight For Their Young
cards.caption: Attenborough narrating mothers hunting eggs for their cubs and rescuing young from abductors. Nobody does the emotional whiplash of nature like BBC Earth. Kept from ~5,000 posts today.

cards.title: The Ocean's Underwater Killers
cards.caption: A full Nat Geo episode on ocean predators that "may appear to..." and then the card cuts off. Solid content, but too generic and low-context to stand out as a repost.

cards.title: 3 Hours Of British Wildlife
cards.caption: Beavers reshaping the land, otters, wetlands to rugged coastlines. A 3-hour slow-TV escape into untold British wildlife. Save this for a rainy Sunday. One of ~50 our feed kept.

cards.title: Deadliest Battles For Survival
cards.caption: Lions, hyenas, cubs in the crossfire: win or die, no second place. The savanna doesn't do participation trophies. Which team are you rooting for? Surfaced by Threadverse.

cards.title: Tiger vs A Wolf Pack In Siberia
cards.caption: A Siberian tiger surrounded by a wolf pack in the snow. Siberia is where the most brutal predators go to refuge, and it shows. Who wins? Kept from ~5,000 posts today.
cards.quote: The Refuge of the Most Brutal Predators

cards.title: An Elephant's Walk Across Africa
cards.caption: Giraffes fighting for dominance, hatchlings racing for their first breath, an elephant crossing a sea of gazelle. Africa in one frame. Threadverse pulled this from the noise.

cards.title: Evolution's Weirdest Flex: The Platypus
cards.caption: A duck's bill, a beaver's tail, venomous spurs, and it lays eggs. Attenborough narrating Tasmania's strangest mammal is peak comfort viewing. What animal breaks your brain most?

cards.title: Lions of the 4K Savanna
cards.caption: A stunning 4K African safari, but the post is mostly a channel pitch with no single moment to anchor on. Threadverse surfaced better lion content this week.

cards.title: The Dinosaur Bird of the Congo
cards.caption: The shoebill stork looks like a dinosaur that forgot to go extinct, and it hunts baby crocodiles. The Congo doesn't play. Would you get within 10 feet of this thing?
cards.povText: POV: a dinosaur survived and it lives in the Congo swamp

cards.title: Two Lions vs One Angry Buffalo
cards.caption: In the wilderness of Niassa, survival is a full-time race. Two lions, one cornered buffalo, and nothing decided until the last second. Who are you betting on?

cards.title: The Ghosts of China's Mountains
cards.caption: Golden fox cubs, hidden kingdoms, creatures most people will never see. Nat Geo calls them the ghosts of China's mountains. Threadverse kept this one out of 5,000.
cards.quote: Who are the ghosts of China's mountains?

cards.title: Two Lions Own the Savanna
cards.caption: Another savanna survival doc from the same channel, near-identical framing to the Niassa clip. Emoji-stuffed title, no distinct moment. Cutting this one.

cards.title: A Cougar Under a Volcano
cards.caption: Under the snowy crest of the High Cascades sit the most active volcanoes in the Lower 48, and a mountain lion prowls right through them. America the Beautiful, indeed.
